title: Inventory source-apportionment studies, satellite PM2.5 products, and ground-monitor networks for LMIC megacities
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates published source-apportionment studies, satellite PM2.5 products, ground-monitor networks, and emission inventories into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC megacity coverage and methodology-comparison assessment.
inputs: Published source-apportionment studies for LMIC megacities
Satellite PM2.5 product documentation (MODIS, VIIRS, TROPOMI)
Emission inventory documentation for LMIC cities
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic coverage, and documented limitations — particularly apportionment-methodology variability and monitor sparsity in LMIC megacities.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering LMIC megacity coverage, apportionment methodology, and monitor-density documentation.

title: Map PM2.5 source-attribution for at least two LMIC megacities with published apportionment studies
outcome: A source-attribution analysis for at least two LMIC megacities using published apportionment studies, documenting methodology choice, source-contribution estimates, seasonal variation, and monitor-density representativeness.
inputs: Published source-apportionment studies for target cities
Satellite PM2.5 spatial data for target cities
expected_artifact: validation.md update with source-attribution methodology and city-level results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms apportionment-study extraction, methodology documentation, and monitor-density representativeness assessment for each target city.
done_condition: Source-attribution analysis includes methodology, city-level results, seasonal variation documentation, monitor representativeness assessment, and replication status for at least two cities.

title: Estimate sector-specific health impacts from source-attributed PM2.5 concentrations
outcome: A sector-specific health-impact analysis applying concentration-response functions to source-attributed PM2.5 for at least two LMIC megacities, with explicit documentation of concentration-response transferability and source-attribution uncertainty propagation.
inputs: population and baseline mortality data for target cities
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with health-impact methodology and sector-specific results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms concentration-response application, uncertainty propagation methodology, and source-attribution-to-health-impact chain documentation.
done_condition: Health-impact analysis includes methodology, sector-specific results, concentration-response transferability caveats, uncertainty propagation, and replication status.

title: Red-team source-attribution overstatement and borrowed-context risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how source-attribution estimates could overstate certainty by ignoring methodology variability, how borrowed-context attributions (from different cities) could mislead policy, and how sector-specific health impacts could be misused for source-specific regulation without adequate evidence.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every source-attribution claim has methodology documentation, monitor-representativeness assessment, and borrowed-context caveats where applicable. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every source-attribution claim is either blocked, softened to methodology-limited language with explicit representativeness caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match air-quality policy and industrial regulation decision needs in LMIC megacities
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the source-attribution analysis would change any real decision for air-quality management agencies, industrial regulators, or transport planners in LMIC megacities without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: air-quality policy context for target cities
industrial and transport regulatory framework documentation
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for air-quality policy and regulatory audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that borrowed-context attributions drive regulation inappropriate for the actual source mix, or that methodology-limited estimates are treated as definitive. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Delhi Pollution Control Committee officer), named decision (e.g., source-specific emission control targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for industrial closure without local source-apportionment evidence), and replicated field-reality review.
